title: 3 Establishing Policies for Processing Trace Requests on OLT
body_text:
3	Establishing Policies for Processing Trace Requests on OLT
3.1	Establishing a Policy on Internal Controls and OLT User Management
Financial institutions are responsible for OLT security practices and internal controls. Some policies may include but arenât limited to:
Ensuring that OLT Tracers and SuperTracers receive their one-time Client Centre passwords in a secure and confidential manner (applies to individuals who donât already have a Client Centre user ID or whose Client Centre password is reset)
Identifying and deactivating inactive OLT Tracers and SuperTracers
Limiting OLT access to employees who require OLT to complete their job requirements
Prohibiting the use of multiple OLT user IDs for an employee
3.2	Establishing a Policy on Authorizing Trace Requests
The financial institution can establish a policy on authorization requirements for new trace requests. If authorization is required, both Tracers and SuperTracers must be created. SuperTracers will authorize trace requests created by Tracers. If authorization of new trace requests isnât required, all OLT users can be set up as SuperTracers.
If the financial institution sets up a decentralized OLT environment, each branch creates, authorizes, and receives trace requests. Although SuperTracers can authorize trace requests for all branches, the financial institution can establish a policy that instructs SuperTracers to authorize only those trace requests that originate at their own branch.
3.3	Establishing a Policy on Processing Biller-Initiated Trace Requests
Since Tracers and SuperTracers from each branch can view all biller-initiated traces, the financial institution should establish policy on whether each branch or a designated branch (e.g., the head office) is responsible for processing biller-initiated trace requests. This policy is particularly important in a partially centralized OLT environment.
See Chapter 4, Choosing an OLT Environment, for more information on partially centralized OLT environments.
END
